name: Kamchatka Krai (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the snows of Kamchatka and the long polar winter
the Pacific Ocean and the Sea of Okhotsk that frame the peninsula
the active volcanoes of Kamchatka and the sun
the silhouettes of the volcanoes and the volcanic ash

description: A horizontal bicolour of a wide white stripe over a narrower blue stripe, with three volcanoes silhouetted against a rising sun in the upper hoist of the white field.
history: Adopted on 5 March 2010 by the Legislative Assembly of Kamchatka Krai, after the merger of the previous Kamchatka Oblast and the Koryak Autonomous Okrug in 2007. The volcano-and-sun emblem comes from the krai's coat of arms.

features.symbolism: white represents the snow and the long Kamchatkan winter; blue represents the Pacific Ocean and the seas surrounding the peninsula.
three volcanoes silhouetted against a rising red sun; drawn from the krai's coat of arms and recalls the volcanic landscape of the Kamchatka peninsula.
